headline: The Bull Case For Arm Holdings (ARM) Could Change Following AI Chip Selloff And Rate Jitters – Learn Why
summary: In recent days, Arm Holdings has been swept up in a wider pullback in AI-focused semiconductor names after Broadcom’s cautious chip outlook and stronger US jobs data prompted investors to reassess growth-sensitive technology exposure. Despite the pressure coming from sector and macro forces rather than new Arm-specific problems, the company sits at the crossroads of booming AI demand, intensifying competition, and fresh regulatory scrutiny of its licensing model. headline: How New U.S. China Chip Export Curbs Will Impact Lam Research (LRCX) Investors
summary: In early June 2026, Lam Research came under pressure as new U.S. restrictions on chip equipment shipments to Chinese customers coincided with a broad semiconductor pullback following Broadcom’s weaker earnings outlook and rising interest-rate worries. Beyond the immediate regulatory and sector shock, the episode underscores how heavily Lam’s fortunes are tied to geopolitical decisions and sentiment across the wider AI and chip supply chain. We’ll now examine how heightened U.S.–China export...
